title: Carbon Offset
summary: A retreat photo looks normal until the bytes after the JPEG footer are inspected.
scenario: An employee under review sent one final image to the team chat before their laptop was seized. The picture itself is harmless, but the file structure suggests it was used as a dead-drop for a plaintext confession. Students should inspect the image footer, recover the appended payload, and extract the hidden flag.
learningObjectives: Recover appended evidence from a seemingly normal media file

artifacts.name: retreat_photo.jpg
artifacts.purpose: Primary image containing appended hidden data

artifacts.name: briefing.txt
artifacts.purpose: Short case note handed to solvers

hints: A JPEG should stop after its normal footer.
Check the tail of the file instead of only viewing the image.

llmHandoff.scope: Only this folder should be needed to build the final challenge bundle.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Create a believable JPEG and append either plaintext or lightly encoded text after the real FFD9 footer.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not change the portal UI or create cross-folder dependencies.
